observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Review, analyze, and reconcile the details of the Other Payables account,
including any abnormal balances; and
b. Prepare the necessary adjustments and maintain subsidiary records to
ensure the fair presentation of accounts.

We recommended Management to immediately effect the necessary
adjustments on the incorrect postings noted to properly reflect the correct balance of
the Donations expense account.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Provide complete documentation and adequate disclosure for the outstanding
obligations certified or charged to Accounts Payable so that the correctness and
validity of related liability accounts can be verified and established in the audit;
b. Take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for
those established transactions with no valid claimants and with non-moving
accounts in order to have a fair presentation of accounts in the financial
statements; and
c. Maintain SL for all accounts showing the details of information.

We recommended the Management to:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Office (GSO) to
coordinate in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in the
recording of supplies and materials and maintaining the prescribed records
to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and
issuance of supplies;
b. require the GSO to maintain Stock Cards and shall consolidate weekly the
RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to be submitted to Municipal
Accountant;
c. require the Municipal Accountant and GSO to reconcile accounting and
property records periodically to bring the inventory accounts into
agreement;
d. instruct the Accounting Office to record expenses only when inventories are
issued based on the weekly consolidated Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS)
together with the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
submitted by the GSO, observing proper use of account titles guided by the
rules provided in Section 4(s), Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
e. direct the Accounting Office to submit the requested distribution list to
Commission on Audit.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to investigate and record unaccounted transactions, make
necessary adjusting entries, and process the cancellation/adjustment of stale checks to
restore accurate cash balances in the Cash in Bank - LCCA accounts.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prioritize the prompt submission of the required Bank Reconciliation Statements
(BRS), along with supporting documentation to enable a timely review of the accuracy
and validity of bank balances and reconciling items.

We recommended that the Management:
a) ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances
in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
b) direct all Accountable Officers to immediately settle their respective Cash
Advances through demand letters.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Strictly implement the retention money provisions under Paragraphs 6.1 to
6.2 of Annex E of IRR of RA 9184; and
b. Require contractors to submit a bank guarantee, irrevocable standby letter
of credit, or surety bond callable on demand if they wish to substitute
retention money, subject to the project being on schedule and satisfactorily
completed.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Assess projects that have been completed for a year and issue a Certificate
of Final Acceptance after ensuring compliance with contract specifications;
and
b. Enforce proper monitoring of the warranty security, ensuring that any
defects are reported and addressed within the warranty period.

We respectfully recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Local Engineer to prepare and submit duly Approved List of all on-
going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be
implemented during the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team
Leader (SA & ATL) at the beginning of the year pursuant to Item No. 2.1 of
COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 for verification. The same
shall be regularly updated during the year in case of additional or new PPAs is
to be implemented in that year, otherwise related expenses will be disallowed in
audit and legal and administrative proceedings may be commence as evidence
may warrant under the circumstances.
b. Promptly comply with the posting requirement thru a standard Project
Signboard as designed; and
c. Submit the duly signed Monthly Monitoring Report and/or a Statement of
Work Accomplishments (SWA) based on the interval of verification (%)
percentage provided for in the Table.

We recommended that Management direct the Offices concerned to ensure the
full implementation of all projects funded by 20% DF within the budget year for
optimum utilization of the fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer
(MPDO) closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different
development projects in accordance with the AIP for timely delivery of basic and
value-added services and benefits to its constituents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to review
and reconcile collections and remittances to determine any unremitted balance or
recording errors and take necessary steps, such as remitting the amount to Pag-IBIG
or adjusting the accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
identify the details of any unremitted premiums and promptly remit them, if
applicable. Additionally, measures should be taken to ensure the accurate and timely
remittance of PhilHealth contributions to prevent future unremitted balances.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRM Officer to:
a.) Review and address the challenges that affected the implementation of the
PPAs funded by the LDRRMF;
b.) Ensure that the planned activities be fully implemented and funds be
efficiently utilized. This will enable the community to fully benefit from the
projects and support the continuous pursuit of disaster risk reduction and
enhanced preparedness and response in the coming years; and
c.) Ensure that Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) are properly categorized
to be charge to the right Disaster Risk Reduction Program following Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRM Officer to strictly
adhere to COA Circular 2012-002 guidelines by submitting the LDRRMFIP that
includes under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the
unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the
Municipal Engineer to ensure the prompt and efficient implementation of
all projects funded under the LGSF-SBDP to optimize the delivery of
services to the constituents; and
b. If the disposition of the unutilized amount is not specified in the MOA, Trust
Agreement, or Memorandum of Understanding, seek an extension from the
RTF-ELCAC for ongoing projects. Alternatively, upon prior consultation
with the RTF-ELCAC and the concerned barangays, facilitate the
immediate reversion of the unutilized balance to the National Treasury.

We commended the Management for its significant compliance with the key
provisions of RA 9003. We encourage the continued observance and adherence to
this law in the future, ensuring sustained environmental responsibility and
stewardship.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to:
a. Strictly adhere to the approved GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that
expenditures align with planned activities; and
b. Enhance monitoring and evaluation mechanisms to track fund utilization and
assess the impact of GAD programs.

We recommended that Management direct HRMO to:
a.) Prepare an Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan, and formulate
policies and guidelines so that the necessity and propriety of hiring job order
employees are justified; and
b.) Review, along with the department heads, the current manpower matrix to
ensure that the hiring and engagement of job orders are in accordance with
the needs of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a. Facilitate the formal transfer of the property to the respective barangays by
executing a Deed of Donation, subject to prior approval from the
Sangguniang Bayan; and
b. Measures should be implemented to ensure future compliance with
regulations governing donated assets.

We recommended the Management to conduct the disposal procedure for the
unserviceable PPE and derecognize the same from the books.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:

   a. MA to record pertinent adjustment to record the donated vehicle;

   b. effect the necessary adjusting entries for the transfer of  the  asset from Trust to General Fund; and


   c. revert the payables related to undelivered motorcycles and adjust  the PPE and depreciation to correct balance.

We recommended that Management require the:


     a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare yearly  a  duly  certified  list  of taxpayers based on the RPTAR showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year, and furnish  the  same to the Municipal Accounting Office at the beginning of every fiscal year; and


     b. Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to recognize the understatement of RPT and SET Receivables as follows:

         General Fund Books


         Real Property Taxes Receivables (1-03-01-020)       ₱13,039,903.66
                     Deferred    Real    Property     Tax     (2-05-01-010)
      ₱13,039,903.66
         Special Education Fund Books

         Special Education Taxes Receivables (1-03-01-03)   ₱13,181,499.89
                   Deferred  Special  Education  Fund   Tax   (2-05-01-020)
      ₱13,181,499.89

        and;


     c. Municipal Accountant to record RPT/SET Receivable yearly  based  on the duly certified list  provided  by  the  MTO,  and  prepare  and maintain the JEVs and the Abstract of RPT Collections in accordance with the provisions of GAAM and Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management immediately submit  the  MOA  or  Trust Agreement for all programs and projects. Henceforth,  ensure  that  the  COA Audit Team is always  furnished  a  copy  of  MOA  or  Trust  Agreement  for validation of transactions involving Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management: (a) instruct the  Municipal  Treasurer to furnish timely relevant data to the  accounting  Office  for  the  proper recording and  recognition  of  receivable  and  income  from  market  stall rentals; and  (b)  direct  the  accountant  to  use  the  accrual  basis  of recognizing revenue from market stall rentals to conform with the PPPSAS  to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that Management evaluate and consider a shift from the manual to automated (ATM) payroll  system in  paying  salaries,  wages  and other benefits of its employees  in  order  to  achieve  a  more  efficient, convenient, accurate, and cost effective internal control  or  safeguard  on payroll.  Payment of salaries directly  to  employees’  bank  accounts  will likely prevent delay  and/or  misappropriation  of  public  funds  that  are inherent in a manual system.


       We also recommended that Management request the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Human Resource  and  Management  Officer  to negotiate with  the  Agency’s  AGSBs  for  the  use  of  their  ATM  service facilities in paying the payroll of the LGU.

We recommended that  management:  (a)  Discontinue  the  practice  of granting cash  advances  to  the  Municipal  Treasurer;  (b)  The  Municipal Treasurer  designate  or  appoint  a  disbursing  officer  to   handle   the disbursing function pursuant to Section  3.1  of  COA  Circular  No.  97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 50 of GAAM, Volume III.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal treasurer  to: (a) deposit her collections intact and daily or  not  later  than  the  next banking day; (b) to cease and desist from and paying expenses directly  from collections; and (c) prohibit/discourage borrowings  and  accommodations  or cash payment of expenses  from  collections,  to  avoid  any suspension or disallowance in audit.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require her collectors  to remit their collections daily in accordance with  Section  29  of  NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended the Management: (a) instruct the Municipal Assessor and other officials concerned to prioritize and facilitate the titling of these parcels of land in order to establish absolute  ownership  and  protect  the legal interest of the LGU thereon; (b)  direct  the  Municipal  Assessor  to appraise the untitled lots to its current market value and provide the  same to the Municipal Accountant for reference in proper recording  in  the  book of accounts; and (c)  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to facilitate  and supervise the proper payments of expenses for the  titling  of  these  lands and to update and reconcile  the  inventory  of  property  assets  with  the updated records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management direct the Offices concerned to  ensure the full implementation of  all  projects  funded  by  20%  DF  for  optimum utilization of  the  fund  to  help  achieve  the  desirable  socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

      We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Planning  and  Development Officer (MPDO) closely  monitor  and  evaluate  the  implementation  of  the different development  projects  in  accordance  with  the  AIP  for  timely delivery  of  basic  and  value-added   services   and   benefits   to   its constituents.

      We recommended further that the Municipal  Budget  Officer  thoroughly examine projects in the continuing  appropriations,  especially  those  that remained unobligated at year-end and  make  representation  with  the  Local Chief  Executive  that  appropriations  no  longer  needed  be   immediately reverted to unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that Management: (a) direct the MDRRMO to  continue  to monitor the implementation of all LDRRM PPAs to fully  achieve  the desired benefits therefrom; and (b)  maximize  utilization  of  available  funds  to strengthen its capability to effectively prepare, respond and  recover  from the adverse effects and impact of natural disasters or calamities.

We recommended that  Management  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to conduct and intensify tax campaigns to maximize  collections  especially  on the delinquent taxpayers and impose remedies provided for, and exercise  the taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that management strictly adhere  with  the  above-cited provisions to ensure that GAD PAPs of the  agency  are  in  consonance  with undertakings  of  the  government  in  in  view  of  the  Covid-19  disease situation.

 We also recommended that management formulate  coherent,  well-defined and specific activities that are responsive to the call  of  the  times  and that will meet the standards and criteria specified in  the  guidelines  set  forth above.

We recommended that  management:  (a)  devise  appropriate  means  and provide incentives to local fisher folks and suppliers to encourage them  to avail  and  make  use  of  the  municipal  fish  port;   (b)   institute   a comprehensive study of  the  operations  of  the  municipal   fish  port  to implement reforms and improvements  thereon; and  (c)  adequately  plan  the PPAs to be implemented by ensuring  that  high-impact  development  projects are prioritized so that the best possible desired  level  of  socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be attained.

We  recommended  the  Management  to:  (a)  stop  practicing   unsound ecological solid waste management; (b)  prioritize  the  completion  of  the sanitary landfill; and  (c)  in  the meantime  that  the  SLF  is  not  yet operational, arrange an agreement with nearby municipality who has  existing waste  disposal  facility  to  temporarily  dispose   the   residual   waste collections of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management  continue  complying  with  RA  No. 8291,  on  the  required  deductions   of   premiums   and   other   related contributions/loans and timely  remittance  thereof  to  the  GSIS  for  the benefit of the employees.
We recommended the Management to continue to observe  the  withholding and prompt remittance of contributions and loan  amortization  due  to  Pag-IBIG.
We  recommended  the  Management  to  ensure  timely   and   accurate remittance  of  PhilHealth  contributions  to  prevent  the  recurrence  of unremitted balances.

We recommended that Management:          
(a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact and daily or the next banking day to the authorized government depository bank as required in Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and other existing regulations to avoid possible theft/loss or misuse; 
(b) order all collectors to remit collections intact to the Municipal Treasurer (MT) on a daily basis; and 
(c) require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile entries in the Cashbook and General Ledger/Subsidiary Ledger at least on a quarterly basis and trace the material variance of ₱7,597,210.36 and any significant findings should be resolved accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Demand Letter for immediate deposit.
observations.reason: Distance of the LGU to AGDBs and unavailability of service vehicle; Location of the collector is far from the MTO Office;
Long outsanding cash advances were inherited balances with incomplete documentations and the accountable officers were long separated from the service

observations.managementAction: a. Small spaces were allocated to be a stockroom per offices
observations.reason: Limited Resources, no budget allocation for the establishment of separate GSO office; Limited manpower

observations.managementAction: Registration of untiled land will commence in 2022
observations.reason: Limited Manpower and difficulty in reconciliation of 2013 and previous year beginning balance; Limited manpower

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 
strictly comply with the allocation of one per cent of IRA to support LCPC programs and activities of the LGU; and 
direct the members of the LCPC to prepare and submit the reports required under DILG MC No. 2012-120 for the proper and effective implementation of projects.

We recommended that Management require: 
the Municipal Accountant to effect the following suggested adjusting entries: 
General Fund 
Dr. Cash in Bank ₱1,512,961.68 
Cr. Government Equity ₱1,512,961.68 
To record reversion of unexpended balance of CYs 2011-2013 DRRMF from Special Trust Fund to General Fund. 
Trust Fund 
Dr. Trust Liability-DRRMF ₱1,512,961.68 
Cr. Cash in Bank ₱1,512,961.68 
To record reversion of unexpended balance of CYs 2011-2013 DRRMF from Special Trust Fund-DRRM to General Fund. 
To ensure that any unutilized balance of MDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund after the lapse of the five-year holding period shall be reverted back to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund. 
That only programs and projects listed in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan and Appropriation Ordinance be utilized under the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management: 
fully implement all uncompleted projects as of December 31, 2019; 
execute all projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s development project; and 
ensure realignment of appropriations shall be made in accordance with Sections 322 and 336 of the RA No. 7160, and in line with other applicable laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Creation of Engr II in 2022 budget
observations.reason: Lack of Manpower, Delay in procurement due to absence of contractor particularly in remote barangay’s

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 
fully utilize the funds granted by the national agencies to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; 
ensure that the Memorandum of Agreements are kept intact so as to properly monitor the agency’s compliance with its specific terms and duration of implementation; and 
liquidate fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects and require the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management: 
direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct and intensify tax campaign and impose penalties, in accordance with law, on delinquent taxpayers to maximize collection; and 
seek the assistance of barangay officials in the delivery of notices of delinquencies and subsequent collection thereof.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Delinquencies are religiously being sent to erring taxpayers. However, their duty ends with the sending of NODs as the taxing authority and final resolve as to these RPT delinquencies lies with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO). The LGU’s Treasury Department is only deputized as collectors. The PTO has the right and the duty under the law to avail of the remedies provided for under the Local Government Code.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:


     a. Municipal Accountant to record RPT/SET Receivable based on  a  duly
        certified    list of taxpayers; and


     b. Municipal Treasurer to designate personnel who shall   handle/focus
        only  with  updating  the  Real  Property  Tax  Account   Registers
        (RPTARs)/Taxpayer’s Index Card (TIC) and at the beginning  of  each
        year, furnish the Municipal Accountant of  a  duly  certified  list
        showing the name of taxpayers and the amount  due  and  collectible
        for the year in accordance with Sec. 20 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that Management: (a) require the  coordination  between
the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the   Municipal   Treasurer   towards   the
implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in  recording  of  supplies
and  materials;  (b)  maintain  the  prescribed  records  to  ensure  proper
accounting and promote  better  control  in  the  receipt  and  issuance  of
supplies; and (c) provide a stock room for proper storage of inventories.

We recommended that Management:


       a. instruct the Inventory Committee  to  include  all  properties  in
          physical count and submit an  updated  and  properly  accomplished
          RPCPPE with all the needed information; and


       b. designate one personnel each  from  the  accounting  and  property
          offices who shall: maintain, on a full time  basis,  the  complete
          and  updated PPE ledger and  property cards for  each  category  of
          PPE indicating adequate details/description of the properties such
          as model, serial number,  location,  cost  and  other  information
          necessary   to   facilitate   identification;    and    facilitate
          reconciliation of records.

We recommended that Management:


      a. strictly  implement  the  anti-littering  policy  and  require  the
         barangays to formulate and implement an Anti-Littering ordinance at
         their level;


      b. encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the
         solid waste management program of the government by educating their
         respective constituents on the proper segregation of wastes;

      c.  enforce  sanctions  on  violators  of  the  ordinances,  laws  and
         regulations pertaining to waste disposal; and


      d. intensify efforts to have the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management  Plan
         finalized by  the  Solid  Waste  Management  Board  to  hasten  the
         approval of  the  plan  by  the  National  Solid  Waste  Management
         Commission.

We  recommended  that  Management  (a)  strictly  comply   with   the
allocation of one per cent of IRA to support LCPC  programs  and  activities
of the LGU; and (b) direct the members of the LCPC  to  prepare  and  submit
the reports  required  under  DILG  MC  No.  2012-120  for  the  proper  and
effective implementation of projects.

We recommended that Management require:

     a.  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  effect  the  following   suggested
        adjusting entries:

      General Fund

      Cash in Bank           ₱1,512,961.68
            Government Equity           ₱1,512,961.68

       To record reversion of unexpended balance of CYs 2011-2013 DRRMF from
      Special Trust Fund-DRRM to General Fund.

      Trust Fund

      Trust Liability – DRRMF     ₱1,512,961.68
            Cash in Bank                ₱1,512,961.68


       To record reversion of unexpended balance of CYs 2011-2013 DRRMF from
      Special Trust Fund-DRRM to General Fund.


     b. the Municipal Accounting  Office  to  ensure  that  any  unutilized
        balance of MDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund after the lapse of  the
        five-year  holding  period  shall   be   reverted   back   to   the
        Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.


     c. that only programs and projects listed  in  the  LDRRMF  Investment
        Plan  and  incorporated  in  the   Annual   Investment   Plan   and
        Appropriation Ordinance be utilized under the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with  GAD  policies  to
implement  GAD  related  activities,  properly  coordinate  with   all   the
personnel/offices involved and ensure proper monitoring of the GAD PPAs,  to
be able to implement all GAD  PPAs  as  planned/budgeted  to  achieve  GAD’s
objectives and address gender issues.

We recommended that Management:

     a. fully implement all uncompleted projects as of December 31, 2019;


     b. execute all projects within the  time  frame  programmed  for  their
        completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents  the
        benefits of the agency’s development project; and

     c. ensure realignment of appropriations shall  be  made  in  accordance
        with Sections 322 and 336 of the RA No. 7160, and in line with other
        applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management: (a) fully utilize  the  funds  granted
by  the  national  agencies  to  effectively  and  timely  extend   to   the
constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; (b)  ensure  that  the
Memorandum of Agreements are kept intact so as to  properly  monitor  the
agency’s compliance with its specific terms and duration of  implementation;
and (c) liquidate fund transfers once projects are fully implemented  and/or
refund the  unutilized  amount  from  completed  projects  and  require  the
appropriate recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management: (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer  to
conduct and intensify tax campaign and impose penalties, in accordance  with
law, on delinquent taxpayers to  maximize  collection;  and   (b)  seek  the
assistance  of  barangay  officials  in   the   delivery   of   notices   of
delinquencies and subsequent collection thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to: coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and depository bank for reconciling items which are doubtful in nature and exert effort to trace the said transactions; ensure that book reconciling items are recorded immediately once the supporting documents are available; and exert effort to trace the amount of ₱43,730.50 intended for deposit but remained undeposited to the bank account for an unreasonable length of time.
observations.reason:
Deposits totaling ₱91,576.40 were not yet recorded in the books as of December 31, 2019.
Deposit in Transit totaling ₱15,000.00 remained uncredited by the bank to the agency account as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: adjust the affected accounts whenever stale checks are discovered, particularly during the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements; and make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks as follows: For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years - CIB-LC, CA  ₱xxx Liability Account       ₱xxx For stale checks over 2 years - CIB-LC, CA  ₱xxx Prior Period Adjustment*      ₱xxx (*to represent surplus adjustment)
observations.reason: State Checks totaling ₱65,487.37 remained unadjusted in the books as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to: submit immediately the original copies of the above 48 cancelled checks; and strictly observe Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs in the proper treatment for cancelled checks.
observations.reason: Coordination with the previous OIC-Municipal Treasurer for the retrieval of the 48 cancelled checks is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record RPT/SET Receivable based on a duly certified list extracted from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card (TIC) showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Sec. 20 of the NGAS Manual. This is also useful in monitoring arrearages as well as benchmark in determining collection efficiency.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
instruct the Inventory Committee to include all properties in physical count and submit an updated and properly accomplished RPCPPE with all the needed information; direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support GL account balances and the GSO to keep corresponding property cards for each category of PPE for control purposes. These records should contain adequate details/description of the properties such as model, serial number, location, cost and other information necessary to facilitate identification; and require both the Accounting Division and Property Section to exert all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC and the concerned officers and employees to: strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and exercise due diligence in the performance of their duties to ensure that all requirements of the procurement law are faithfully adhered to.

We recommended that Management ensure: the full implementation of the 20% development fund embodied in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality in compliance with Paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1; and that any change in the annual budget must be made through a supplemental budget duly enacted in accordance with the requirements under Section 321 of the Local Government Code, not by mere resolution. Any re-appropriation of funds reverted must likewise be made through an ordinance in accordance with Section 322 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management: fully utilize the funds granted by the national agencies to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; ensure that the Memorandum of Agreements are kept intact so as to properly monitor the agency’s compliance with its specific terms and duration of implementation; and record the grant of ₱10,000,000.00 in the Due to NGAs account.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the Municipality in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities; create new positions and provide more additional capable personnel that will cause the issuance of NOD and the monitoring of the delinquent tax payers; and intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

   a. coordinate with  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  depository  bank  for
      reconciling items which are doubtful in nature  and  exert  effort  to
      trace the said transactions;


   b. ensure that book reconciling items are recorded immediately  once  the
      supporting documents are available; and


   c. exert effort to trace the amount of ₱43,730.50  intended  for  deposit
      but remained undeposited to  the  bank  account  for  an  unreasonable
      length of time.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a) adjust the affected  accounts  whenever  stale  checks  are  discovered,
   particularly during the preparation of Bank  Reconciliation  Statements;
   and
b) make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks as follows:

    a. For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years -

      CIB-LC, CA             Pxxx
           Liability Account                 Pxxx

    b. For stale checks over 2 years -


      CIB-LC, CA             Pxxx
           Prior Period Adjustment*          Pxxx
           (*to represent surplus adjustment)

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:


     a. submit immediately the original copies of the  above  48  cancelled
        checks; and


 b.    strictly observe Section 59 of NGAS Manual for  LGUs  in  the  proper
    treatment for cancelled checks.

We recommended that Management require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
record RPT/SET Receivable based on a duly certified list extracted from  the
Real Property Tax  Account  Register  (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s  Index  Card  (TIC)
showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due  and  collectible  for
the year in accordance with Sec. 20  of  the  NGAS  Manual.   This  is  also
useful  in  monitoring  arrearages  as  well  as  benchmark  in  determining
collection efficiency.

We recommended that Management:


       a. instruct the Inventory Committee  to  include  all  properties  in
          physical count and submit an  updated  and  properly  accomplished
          RPCPPE with all the needed information;


       b. direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete  and  updated
          PPE ledger cards to support GL account balances  and  the  GSO  to
          keep corresponding property cards for each  category  of  PPE  for
          control  purposes.   These   records   should   contain   adequate
          details/description  of  the  properties  such  as  model,  serial
          number,  location,  cost  and  other  information   necessary   to
          facilitate identification; and


       c. require both the Accounting Division and Property Section to exert
          all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so  that
          discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management  require  the  BAC  and  the  concerned
officers and employees to:


   a. strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in Revised  IRR  of  RA
      No. 9184; and
   b. exercise due diligence in the performance of their  duties  to  ensure
      that all requirements of the procurement law  are  faithfully  adhered
      to.

We recommended that Management ensure:

   a. the full implementation of the 20% development fund embodied  in  the
      Annual  Investment  Plan  of  the  Municipality  in  compliance  with
      Paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1; and


   b. that any  change  in  the  annual  budget  must  be  made  through  a
      supplemental budget duly enacted in accordance with the  requirements
      under  Section  321  of  the  Local  Government  Code,  not  by  mere
      resolution. Any re-appropriation of funds reverted must  likewise  be
      made through an ordinance in accordance with Section 322 of the Local
      Government Code.

We recommended that Management: (a) fully utilize  the  funds  granted
by  the  national  agencies  to  effectively  and  timely  extend   to   the
constituents the benefits of the agency’s  projects;  (b)  ensure  that  the
Memorandum of Agreements are kept intact  so  as  to  properly  monitor  the
agency’s compliance with its specific terms and duration of  implementation;
and (c) record the grant of ₱10,000,000.00 in the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended that Management:

a. re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials  to  aid
   the Municipality in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions
   and responsibilities;

b. create new positions and provide more additional capable personnel  that
   will cause the issuance of NOD and the monitoring of the delinquent  tax
   payers; and

c. intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury  personnel  to  barangays
   especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order
   to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

We recommended that Management continue to withhold the payment of salaries and all other emoluments of erring accountable officers to fully recover the undeposited collections, otherwise institute appropriate legal sanctions against erring accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Continuous withholding of Terminal Leave, Salaries and other emoluments of Accountable Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare and provide all the information needed in the RPCPPE as well as certify the same and submit a copy to COA within the prescribed period; require the General Services Officer/Property Officer to reconcile the property records of each class of PPE with the accounting records, investigate the differences and adjustments be effected immediately; and direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support GL account balances and the GSO to keep corresponding property cards for each category of PPE for control purposes which shall contain adequate details/description of the properties such as model, serial number, location, cost and other information necessary to facilitate its identification.
observations.managementAction: Reconcile book records vs actual physical inventory records

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) strictly comply with the allocation of one per cent of IRA to support LCPC programs and activities of the LGU; and (b) direct the members of the LCPC to prepare and submit the reports required under DILG MC No. 2012-120 for the proper and effective implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: Actual Appropriation for LCPC per 2018 Budget is ₱245,100.00 however other amounts in the approved budget is used for LCPC such as but not limited to ECCD/DCW budget, conduct of 2018 regional nutrition summit, fencing of ECCD building.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) fully implement all uncompleted projects as of December 31, 2017; (b) instruct the Offices of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget and Municipal Planning and Development to coordinate closely in the monitoring of the implementation of the projects funded from the 20% Economic Development Fund; (c) execute all projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s development projects; and (d) open a separate depository account for the 20% EDF to facilitate monitoring of the cash in bank account and to prevent unauthorized disbursements from the fund.
We recommended that Management fully utilize the funds granted by the national agencies to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects.

observations.reason: Failure of bidding due to unavailability of qualified supplier

whereabouts and provide documentation thereon for the remaining umaccounted
2,792.12 kilos or 52 sacks of rice and 19,482 packs of noodles which both did not
appear in the Inventory Report of Undistributed Relief Goods; (b) ensure that the
remaining 16,048 cans of sardines will be distributed pursuant to the purpose for

mechanisms that will aid the LGU in the proper distribution and monitoring of all
the relief goods acquired either by way of purchase or donation; (d) determine the
adequate quantity of relicf goods necessary to respond for the immediate needs of
the constituents while avoiding excessive procurement thereof which may result to
wastage especially for perishable goods; and (e) review the provisions of RA No.
10121 especially the 13 prohibited acts under Section 19 thereof.

We recommended. that Management: (a) execute all projects within the time -
frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the
constituents the benefits of the agency’s development projects; and (b) instruct the
Offices of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget and Municipal Planning and
Development to coordinate closely in the monitoring of the implementation of the
projects funded from the 20% Economic Development Fund.

by the national agencies t;o effectively and timely extend to the constituents the

observations.managementAction: a. Issued Executive Order creating and mandating the Inventory Committee to conduct annual inventory count of all asset.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that: (a) in their preparation of LDRRMFIP, provide for  projects and activities which will be charged not only against the ocurrent appropriation for LDRRMF but also against the prior years’ unexpended balance of the said fund; and (b) fully irfiplenient all the projects and + activities programmed in’ the annual LDRRMFIP so as not to deprive their constituents of the benefits that would be derived from the maximum utilization thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Unexpended Calamity Fund is part of the 30% Quick Response Fund (QRF). This amount is not presented under QRF in the 2016 Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed’ that the process and implement appropriate steps that would lead to the closure of the open dump and switch to samitary landfill; (b) intensify efforts to achieve and embrace the full concept of Ecological solid waste management and adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003; () discontinue the practice of collecting non-segregated waste and come up with measures that would help its cause. Encouraging waste - segregation at source will also be beneficial in transitioning from an open dumpsite to a sanitary land fill as it would lessen the amount of wastes that would go to this dumpsite; and (d) adopt scheduling method/scheme which would allow the collection of different types of wastes on different days.

We rtecommended. and the Management agreed’ that the GAD  Focal  Person, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, prepare GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2015, that include the implemented  activities which were streamlined ‘with the agency’s other programs and submit the same to the COA. We further recommended and the Management also, agreed to provide the oversight agencies copies of the approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget and the prescribed Accomplishment Report _on GAD. and its supporting documents to comply with the requirements of the law.
observations.managementAction: a. 2016 Plan and Accomplishment Report and prepared and copies were already submitted to the oversight agencies in accordance  with the requirements prescribed by law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) fully implement all  uncompleted 20% projects - as of December 31, 2015 for both under the current_and continuing appropriation; (b) instruct the Offices of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget and Municipal Planning and Development to coordinate closely in the monitoring of the implementation of the projects funded from the 20% Economic Development Fund; and (c) execute all projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to efféctively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s development projects.
